name: Engineering (Non-Software)
description: You build things that have to work safely in the real world — where failure isn't a bug report, it's a disaster. AI is being deployed with almost none of the safety engineering discipline your field developed over centuries.
trajectory: Safety margins, redundancy, and fail-safes map directly to AI safety engineering and robustness.
Testing to destruction and stress analysis translates to AI stress testing and adversarial evaluation.
Standards compliance and certification connects to AI safety standards and certification — which barely exist yet.
Your instinct for identifying gaps in safety practices is obvious to any engineer but invisible to people who've never built something that could hurt someone.

projects.title: Compare engineering and AI safety standards
projects.description: Read AI safety testing practices and identify which engineering standards from your discipline the system would fail.

projects.title: Import safety culture to AI
projects.description: Write about what AI development can learn from safety engineering in your discipline — near-miss reporting, certification, fail-safes.

projects.title: Physical AI safety governance
projects.description: Contribute to safety standards for AI in robotics, vehicles, and industrial systems, drawing on mature engineering practices.
